description: Fix bugs found during plugin audit: stale SDK version comment in indeterminate-progress tool, and missing fetch timeout in testApi wrapper
qualityChecks: cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove stale SDK version comment and use optional progress fields in indeterminate-progress tool
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the indeterminate-progress tool to use the SDK's optional progress fields correctly so that the code accurately reflects the current SDK API and doesn't mislead future developers
acceptanceCriteria: The comment referencing 'The published SDK (v0.0.16)' is removed from plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/indeterminate-progress.ts
The reportProgress calls omit progress and total fields (relying on ProgressOptions optionality) instead of passing sentinel values { progress: 0, total: 0 }
The tool still reports 3 progress steps with message strings ('Step 1: Initializing...', 'Step 2: Processing...', 'Step 3: Finishing...')
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/indeterminate-progress.ts (lines 14-27).

The current code passes { progress: 0, total: 0, message: '...' } to reportProgress, with a stale comment on lines 15-19 explaining this was needed because the SDK v0.0.16 had progress/total as required fields. The current SDK (v0.0.42) has both fields as optional in the ProgressOptions interface (see platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts lines 18-25). The fix is:
1. Remove the comment block on lines 15-19
2. Change the three reportProgress calls to only pass { message: '...' } — omitting progress and total entirely to test the true indeterminate case

The E2E tests (e2e/) verify this tool's behavior — the tests should already handle indeterminate progress correctly since the adapter runtime fills in defaults.

userStories.title: Add fetch timeout to testApi wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer running E2E tests, I want testApi calls to fail fast with a descriptive timeout error when the test server is unresponsive, so that I get clear error messages instead of tools hanging for minutes
acceptanceCriteria: The fetch call in testApi (plugins/e2e-test/src/test-api.ts) uses AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) to enforce a 30-second timeout
When the timeout fires, a ToolError.timeout is thrown with a descriptive message mentioning the endpoint and timeout duration
When the abort signal fires (non-timeout abort), a ToolError with code 'aborted' is thrown
Network errors are caught and wrapped in a ToolError with code 'network_error', category 'internal', and retryable: true
The existing error handling for non-ok HTTP responses and ok:false API responses is preserved unchanged
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/e2e-test/src/test-api.ts (lines 17-21).

The current fetch call on line 17 has no timeout:
```
const response = await fetch(endpoint, {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify(body),
});
```

Follow the same pattern used by the Slack plugin's slackApi (plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts lines 389-412):
1. Create an AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) before the fetch
2. Pass it as the signal option
3. Wrap the fetch in try-catch
4. In the catch block, check for TimeoutError DOMException → throw ToolError.timeout
5. Check signal.aborted → throw ToolError with code 'aborted'
6. Otherwise throw ToolError with code 'network_error', category 'internal', retryable: true

ToolError is already imported at the top of the file. Keep the existing error handling after the fetch (lines 23-41) unchanged — only wrap the fetch call itself.
